column3: Two conflicting definitions of Scenario 1.2 (MTP opening revenue). Filter was incorrectly written as (start_date < prev_start OR start_date >= m_start) instead of simply start_date >= m_start (future cycles only).
column6: Opening revenue was inflated/incorrect for months with MTP cases. Required multiple query re-writes.

column3: If a return is placed mid-month (e.g. Dec 12), the system creates future cycles with recognised_at in Dec and start_date in Jan/Feb. The calculation skips the 2nd MTP cycle due to window misalignment.
column6: December opening revenue understated for customers who returned between Dec 1–15. Cannot auto-detect without item-level reconciliation.
column7: Business validation session needed. Provide test case customer IDs with mid-month returns to verify correct expected values.

column3: Confirming TTO churn requires joining rent_to_purchase_items → rent_to_purchase_orders and inspecting payment_details JSON for 'paid'. Attachments need a separate path. Initial approach caused fan-out (duplicate rows). Two SQL files exist (tto_integration.sql tto_fix.sql) as a result.
column4: Partially Fixed — not merged into main
column6: TTO partial vs. full classification producing incorrect counts. Fix tested but not yet validated end-to-end.
column7: Merge tto_fix.sql logic into main automation after validation.

column3: Attachment churn can fire via (a) direct return_attachments record or (b) parent item returned via composite_item_id. Initial logic only handled (a). Fix using COALESCE is fragile — composite_item_id linkage not always populated.
column6: Partial pickup revenue (Component 7) understated for customers who returned items with attachments.
column7: Data team to confirm composite_item_id population rules. Validate against known return cases.

column3: Component 16 requires exploding discount JSON array isolating UPFRONT discounts LAG-comparing consecutive schedules and firing only when previous_tenure ≠ current_tenure. Discount JSON structure varies across records. gap_analysis.sql shows reconciliation logic not yet aligned with manual calculations.
column6: Closing revenue ≠ next month's opening revenue. Bridge gap (Component 32) stays open until this is resolved.
column7: Schedule alignment session with revenue/finance owner to agree on exact formula and validate with 3–5 known plan transition cases.

column2: Revenue Bridge Gap Not Closing to Zero
column3: Bridge validation metric: Gap = Next Month Opening − This Month Closing should be ~zero. Currently non-zero. Cannot identify which component is wrong without running the full bridge for 2 months simultaneously with item-level drill-down.
column6: Entire automation output untrustworthy for stakeholder reporting.
column7: Resolve Issues 2 and 5 first. Then run dual-month bridge on a known stable month (e.g. Jan 2026) and drill down by component.

column3: Parts of automation use Databricks SQL (INSTR() get_json_object() ADD_MONTHS() INTERVAL 330 MINUTES) while others use Redshift syntax (json_extract_path_text() CONVERT_TIMEZONE()). Causes silent failures or wrong results when run on the wrong platform.
column6: Every new component requires manual syntax translation doubling debugging effort. Some queries silently return wrong results on incorrect platform.
column7: Confirm single target platform (Databricks or Redshift) with data engineering team. Standardise all CTEs to that platform's syntax.

column3: Discount field in revenue_recognition_schedules.monetary_components is a JSON array. Exploding it for UPFRONT isolation causes one-to-many row multiplication. If deduplication (dense rank / distinct) is not applied at the right grain revenue amounts are double-counted.
column6: Discount-dependent components (C16 Plan Transition C21 Discount Change C20 Discount Given) may produce inflated totals.
column7: Add per-entity grain validation test: sum of exploded discounts must equal original JSON total. Flag mismatches before using in bridge.

column2: 20+ Components in Single Query — No Modular Testing
column3: Revenue bridge has ~20 active components all in one parameterised query. No modular testing framework. Any one broken component breaks the entire output. Testing requires a full end-to-end run per cycle.
column6: No way to ship partial progress. Cannot isolate which component is failing without running everything.
column7: Break query into independently runnable component CTEs. Agree with team on a test month with known expected values per component.
